Title: Precision Biomanufacturing with Lactic Acid Bacteria: From Ancestral Fermentations to Technological Innovation and Future Prospects for Next-Generation Functional Foods
Authors: Ana Yanina Bustos; Carla Luciana Gerez
AbstractMini: Reviews the shift from conventional LAB fermentation to precision biomanufacturing using CRISPR-Cas editing, multi-omics and AI/ML for rational strain design, reporting metabolic flux engineering for nutraceuticals such as NMN and N-acetylglucosamine.
WhyRelevant: Provides a roadmap for integrating synthetic-biology and AI tools into LAB strain development, directly transferable to brewing biotech (e.g., sour beer, bioprotection, functional metabolite production).

Title: Structural deviations between native and precision fermentation-derived food proteins: Techno-functionality and structure formation strategies
Authors: J. K. Keppler; M. W. T. Werten; R. M. Boom
AbstractMini: Reviews structural differences (N-terminal extensions, glycosylation variants, impurities) between recombinant and animal-derived dairy, egg and gelatin proteins from precision fermentation, and proposes top-down/bottom-up strategies to achieve equivalent food functionality.
WhyRelevant: Maps techno-functional gaps that must be closed before PF-derived proteins can serve as drop-in replacements in food/beverage matrices, including emulsification and gelation relevant to brewing adjuncts.

Title: Postbiotics in Functional Foods: Microbial Derivatives Shaping Health, Immunity and Next-Generation Nutrition
Authors: Alice Njolke Mafe; Javad Sharifi-Rad; Daniela Calina; Adedoyin J. Ogunyemi; Ademola O. Tubi
AbstractMini: Systematically reviews postbiotic sources, mechanisms (immune modulation, metabolic regulation, gut–brain signalling), health benefits, formulation strategies and EFSA/FDA regulatory landscape, arguing postbiotics address key probiotic limitations of viability and shelf stability.
WhyRelevant: Positions postbiotics as shelf-stable functional ingredients for beverages, overcoming live-culture viability challenges in pasteurised or processed drinks—directly relevant to NOLO functional beverage development.

Title: Production of Gluten-Free Craft Beers of High Antioxidant and Sensory Quality
Authors: Antonietta Baiano; Teresa De Pilli; Anna Fiore
AbstractMini: Discusses technical routes for producing gluten-free craft beer while maintaining sensory quality, and reports a formulation/processing approach that preserves antioxidant properties alongside acceptable flavor.
WhyRelevant: Supports gluten-reduction strategy design (enzymes/process) without compromising flavor/claim substantiation.
Owner: MANUEL AGUINAGA CABREDO;LUIS MIGUEL BARRERA ARBELAEZ

Title: Screening of Lactic Acid Bacteria and RSM-Based Optimization for Enhancing γ-Aminobutyric Acid (GABA) Accumulation in Orange Juice
Authors: Shufeng Yin; Yiyao Wang; RuiXue Zhao; Ning Zhao; Hao Liu; Yining Tang; Ningbo Qin; Yiwei Dai; Xinping Lin
AbstractMini: Screens 52 LAB strains and applies response-surface methodology to optimise GABA accumulation in fermented orange juice, achieving 0.89 g/L (+39 % vs. control) using a Lcb. paracasei / Lcb. rhamnosus co-culture.
WhyRelevant: Transferable optimisation framework for GABA enrichment in beverage matrices; LAB strain-selection logic and RSM protocol can be adapted for beer/malt-based functional beverages.

Title: Environmental and economic impacts of using brewers spent grains for animal feed and anaerobic digestion
Authors: Nicholas Davison; Christos Christodoulou; David Humphries; Kirsty Kliem; Sokratis Stergiadis; Laurence Smith
AbstractMini: Quantifies environmental and economic trade-offs of directing brewers' spent grains to animal feed versus anaerobic digestion, using life-cycle and cost analyses to inform valorization pathway selection.
WhyRelevant: Provides decision-support data for breweries choosing between feed and biogas valorization routes, directly mapping cost, GHG and nutrient-recovery trade-offs.

Title: Natural deep eutectic solvents for polyphenol extraction from brewer's spent grain: comparative evaluation with conventional solvents
Authors: Luca Masiello; Giuseppina Adiletta; Matteo Di Renzo; Anna Maria Tarola; Paola Russo
AbstractMini: Compares natural deep eutectic solvents (NADES) against conventional organic solvents for extracting polyphenols from brewer's spent grain, evaluating yield, antioxidant activity and environmental footprint.
WhyRelevant: Provides a direct green-solvent benchmark for BSG polyphenol extraction, relevant to scaling NADES-based processes for brewery circular-economy schemes.

Title: Energy-efficient production of plasma-activated water insights into controllable peroxynitrite chemistry
Authors: D. Gan;L. Hong;S. Yuan;M. Zhu;Y. Gao;T. Zhang;T. Li;B. Chen;A. Dzimitrowicz;P. Jamroz;P. J. Cullen;R. Zhou
AbstractMini: Producción de plasma activated water con control químico del peroxinitrito y eficiencia energética.
WhyRelevant: Relevante para evaluar viabilidad operativa y coste energético de PAW.

Title: Aging of Citra®, Cascade, and Centennial hops under commercial storage and the discrepancies between sensory quality and HSI classification
Authors: Jobe C. A.;Rubottom L. N.; Shellhammer T. H.
AbstractMini: Investigates hop aging of Citra®, Cascade and Centennial under commercial storage conditions and reveals that the Hop Storage Index does not consistently align with trained-panel sensory quality assessments.
WhyRelevant: Challenges the reliance on HSI as sole quality metric for hop storage, providing evidence for integrating sensory panels into QA decisions on raw material shelf life.

Title: Plasma Activated Water in Enhancing Food Safety and Quality A Comprehensive Review
Authors: Shivangi Srivastava;Ubaida Akbar;Md Sabir Ahmed Mondol;Kshirod Kumar Dash;Pir Mohammad Junaid;Madiya Manzoor;Shafat Ahmad Khan;Aamir Hussain Dar
AbstractMini: Reviews plasma-activated water (PAW) as a non-thermal disinfectant in food processing, covering antimicrobial mechanisms, effects on nutritional quality, and regulatory considerations.
WhyRelevant: Directly applicable to brewery CIP and surface sanitation; PAW offers chemical-free disinfection compatible with food-contact surfaces and process water reuse.

Title: Recent trends in non-thermal plasma and plasma activated water Effect on quality attributes mechanism of interaction and potential application in food and agriculture
Authors: Sunil Pipliya;Sitesh Kumar;Nikhil Babar;Prem Prakash Srivastav
AbstractMini: Comprehensive open-access review of non-thermal plasma (NTP) and PAW covering reactive species generation, microbial inactivation mechanisms, and effects on food quality attributes.
WhyRelevant: Comparative framework for risk-benefit analysis of plasma technologies vs conventional CIP; relevant to evaluating novel sanitation alternatives in brewing.

Title: A comprehensive review on cold plasma applications in the food industry
Authors: Raju Sasikumar;Selva Kumar T.;Irengbam Barun Mangang;G. Kaviarasu;Ravinder Kaushik;Paul Mansingh;Vidisha Tomer;Amit K. Jaiswal
AbstractMini: Review de aplicaciones de cold plasma en industria alimentaria enfocado en inactivación microbiana y calidad.
WhyRelevant: Base sólida para evaluar NTP y plasma en sanitización industrial.

Title: Interactions Between Fermentation Temperature and Yeast Strain: Impacts on Polyfunctional Thiol Release and Beer Aroma
Authors: Rania S. Samia; Cédric Chenot; Aaron Shayevitz; Timo Fischborn; Thomas H. Shellhammer
AbstractMini: Examines how fermentation temperature (15–30 °C) and five commercial yeast strains interact to modulate polyfunctional thiol (3SH, 4MSP, 3S4MP) release in pilot-scale Cascade-hopped beers, reporting temperature-dependent 3SH increases of 33–72 %.
WhyRelevant: Directly actionable for brewers tuning fermentation temperature and yeast selection to maximise tropical thiol expression in hop-forward beers.

Title: Assessing the effectiveness of a novel bacteriophage in preventing biofilm formation by the beer-spoilage bacterium Pediococcus damnosus on stainless steel surface
Authors: M. A. Abouelkheir; M. A. Abdallah; E. M. Ismail; M. A. El-Sayed; et al.
AbstractMini: Evaluates a bacteriophage intervention to prevent Pediococcus damnosus biofilm formation on stainless steel, assessing efficacy under relevant conditions for brewery hygiene control.
WhyRelevant: Alternative biofilm-control lever (phages) for brewery-relevant spoilers; useful for non-chemical mitigation concepts and for understanding practical constraints on deployment.

Title: Intelligent industrial cleaning: a multi-sensor approach utilising machine learning-based regression
Authors: Alessandro Simeone; Elliot Woolley; Josep Escrig; Nicholas James Watson
AbstractMini: Presents a multi-sensor industrial cleaning setup and applies machine-learning regression to estimate cleaning state/performance, enabling smarter end-point detection and resource-efficient operation.
WhyRelevant: Relevant for digitising CIP: shifts validation from fixed recipes to state-based control, potentially reducing over-cleaning (water/chemicals/time) while managing hygiene risk.

Title: Is cleaning in place (CIP) ready for the future? - A critical assessment
Authors: Arjan J. Van Asselt; Holly A. Huellemeier; Harald J Schuten; Wolfgang Augustin
AbstractMini: Reviews CIP unit operations and cleaning chemistries, then synthesises environmental impact drivers (water, energy, chemicals, wastewater) and mitigation options including alternative disinfectants and process monitoring.
WhyRelevant: Good baseline to structure a 'CIP alternatives' roadmap with a defensible LCA/footprint framing and a map of levers beyond chemistry alone.

Title: Enhancing clean-in-place system sustainability with recurrent neural network and multi-objective optimization techniques
Authors: R. Sislian; P. R. P. Allegro; R. Gedraite; G. O. Meloni; P. R. S. Oliveira; F. V. Silva; D. L. Toufen
AbstractMini: Uses lab-scale CIP data to train an LSTM model and applies NSGA-II multi-objective optimisation to reduce rinse-water consumption under pulsed/variable flow setpoints; reports substantial water and time reductions vs constant-flow baselines.
WhyRelevant: Practical pathway to reduce CIP water footprint using soft sensors + optimisation, with quantified trade-offs (RMSE vs litres/time) and a validation approach.

Title: Plasma-activated water in agriculture, food safety, and healthcare
Authors: Nguyen Van Duc Long;Sourjo Sengupta; Changping Zhuang; Katharina Richter; Volker Hessel
AbstractMini: Reviews plasma-activated water (PAW): generation methods, reactive species chemistry, stability and application evidence across agriculture, food safety/disinfection and healthcare.
WhyRelevant: Evidence base for PAW as a chemical-lean antimicrobial and cleaning adjunct; useful to benchmark PAW vs electrolyzed water and to identify the real constraints (species stability, dose, material compatibility).

Title: Fungal foam: Effect of different substrates on the growth performance, process efficiency, and material properties
Authors: Nur Mawaddah Majib; Noorulnajwa Diyana Yaacob; Sam Sung Ting; Nor Munirah Rohaizad
AbstractMini: Compares fungal-foam/mycelium-material production on different substrates, quantifying effects on growth performance, process efficiency and resulting material properties relevant to scale-up and application fit.
WhyRelevant: Directly relevant for exploring mycelium-based materials from agro-industrial side streams (including bagasse) and for setting screening KPIs (growth rate, yield, mechanical properties).

Title: EMS-induced mutagenesis of Aspergillus terreus for enhanced xylanase production and subsequent biotransformation of sugarcane bagasse-derived xylose towards xylitol
Authors: Satwika Das; Ramandeep Kaur; Naveen Kumar Bhukya; Kanimozhi Jeyaram; Ashish A. Prabhu
AbstractMini: Applies EMS mutagenesis to improve xylanase production in Aspergillus terreus, then uses the enzyme to saccharify bagasse and biotransforms the resulting xylose to xylitol with Yarrowia lipolytica, reporting titre and yield figures.
WhyRelevant: End-to-end blueprint from bagasse to a value-added polyol (xylitol), including strain improvement, hydrolysis and biotransformation steps.
Owner: RUBEN VERA DOMINGO;EDUARDO BARBOSA GORDO;JORDI ROYES BUISAN

Title: Valorization of Sugarcane Bagasse Via Pre-Pyrolysis Fermentation with Pleurotus ostreatus: Thermal Kinetics and Product Quality Enhancement
Authors: Budianto Budianto; Rinny Ermiyanti Yasin
AbstractMini: Uses Pleurotus ostreatus fermentation as a biological pretreatment before pyrolysis of sugarcane bagasse; reports reduced activation energy, earlier thermal decomposition, and changes in product yields and biochar quality.
WhyRelevant: Uses Pleurotus ostreatus fermentation as a biological pretreatment before pyrolysis of sugarcane bagasse; reports reduced activation energy, earlier thermal decomposition, and changes in product yields and biochar quality.
Owner: JORDI ROYES BUISAN;EDUARDO BARBOSA GORDO;RUBEN VERA DOMINGO

Title: Purification, and characterization of xylanase by Aspergillus krugeri AUMC 15912 utilizing sugarcane bagasse under solid-state fermentation conditions
Authors: Aya A. A. Abdel-Hafeez; Abdel-Raouf M. Khallil; Elhagag A. Hassan; Osama A. M. Al-Bedak
AbstractMini: Optimises SSF production of xylanase on sugarcane bagasse, then purifies and characterises the enzyme (molecular weight, pH/temperature optima, kinetics and inhibitor/salt sensitivity).
WhyRelevant: Provides an SSF + enzyme-performance package for bagasse upgrading; relevant if you want enzymatic hydrolysis, prebiotics or downstream bioconversions.

Title: Mycosynthesis of Silica Nanoparticles from Sugarcane Bagasse Using Aspergillus niger MSF3: Optimization and Economic Assessment
Authors: Mohini Yadav; Nancy George; Vagish Dwibedi
AbstractMini: Demonstrates fungal-mediated conversion of sugarcane bagasse into silica nanoparticles using Aspergillus niger, optimises key parameters via RSM, and provides a basic economic assessment suggesting lower cost than commercial SiNPs.
WhyRelevant: Shows a credible route from agro-waste to functional inorganic nanomaterials; useful for scouting high-margin valorisation options beyond energy/animal feed.

Title: Recent advances in chemical recycling and upcycling of plastic waste into valuable materials, chemicals, and energy: a comprehensive review
Authors: K. Jan; D. Deka; E. Yu; T. Lu; K. Li; H. Jiang; K. Nawaz; E. Webb; S. Ozcan; R. Zou; L. Dai; H. Lei; R. Ruan; X. Zhao; W. Chen
AbstractMini: Comprehensive review of solvent dissolution, catalytic depolymerisation and thermochemical routes for mixed plastics, emphasising selectivity drivers, contaminant handling, scale-up constraints and LCA/TEA considerations.
WhyRelevant: Informs packaging end‑of‑life strategy and design‑for‑recycling decisions by clarifying which chem‑recycling pathways are realistic under contamination and cost constraints.

Title: Rapid and Non-destructive Ethanol Quantification in Beer Using FTIR-ATR Spectroscopy and PLSR Modeling
Authors: Fatos Rexhepi; Dijana Blazhekovikj-Dimovska; Bahtir Hyseni; Artan Gashi
AbstractMini: Demonstrates that FTIR-ATR combined with partial least-squares regression accurately quantifies ethanol content in beer without sample preparation, proposing a rapid non-destructive QC method.
WhyRelevant: Validates a low-cost, rapid spectroscopic alternative to reference methods for routine ethanol QC in production, directly applicable to brewery in-line monitoring.

Title: Treatment of landfill leachate and production of geopolymer using electrocoagulation sludge
Authors: A. Aşkın; H. K. Kırıkbıçak; E. Arıöz; Ü. Tezcan Ün
AbstractMini: Optimises electrocoagulation for high‑strength leachate and demonstrates valorisation of Al‑rich EC sludge into fly‑ash geopolymers, reducing disposal burden.
WhyRelevant: Gives a circular template: effluent polishing + sludge-to-material valorisation, useful for reducing total treatment cost and waste liabilities.

Title: Struvite precipitation from source-separated human urine: mineralogical characterization, phosphorus release kinetics, and heavy metal safety assessment
Authors: Ricardo Franci Gonçalves; Regiane Pereira Roque; Yuri Nascimento Nariyoshi; Renata Estevam; Honerio Coutinho de Jesus
AbstractMini: Characterises struvite recovered from stored urine (mineralogy, phosphorus release kinetics) and assesses heavy‑metal safety to judge fertiliser suitability.
WhyRelevant: Directly informs nutrient recovery programmes: product quality, release behaviour and compliance risks when converting high‑P side streams into fertiliser.

Title: Biofilm Properties and Their Implications for Cleaning Processes in the Food Industry – A Review
Authors: Caroline Bachlechner; Elena Zand; Vincent Eisenrauch; Marc Mauermann; Henry Jäger; Felix Schottroff
AbstractMini: Reviews how biofilm structure, rheology and multispecies composition drive cleanability, and why bulk soil metrics often fail to predict CIP outcomes; highlights shear history and matrix heterogeneity as key determinants of resistance.
WhyRelevant: Improves CIP design/validation by translating 'biofilm physics' into practical levers (sequencing, shear, chemistry) and risk-based verification metrics.

Title: Valorization of brewing by-products: obtaining flavor-enhancing and antioxidant compounds from spent yeast
Authors: Antonela G. Garzón; Yanina Pavón; Marilin E. Aquino; Silvina R. Drago
AbstractMini: Uses response-surface optimisation of autolysis time and temperature to recover flavour‑enhancing compounds from spent brewer’s yeast, balancing umami-related free amino acids with phenolics/antioxidant activity and sensory side-notes.
WhyRelevant: Actionable route to convert BSY into a controllable flavour ingredient; provides optimisation logic and measurable targets for sensory + functionality.

Title: Valorisation of brewery spent grain for biogas production and nutrient-enriched bioslurry in sustainable agriculture
Authors: Tewodros Mulu; Bilhate Chala; Yezbie Kassa; Bernhard Freyer; Tarekegne Fentie; Marye Mulugeta; Dessie Tibebe
AbstractMini: Reviews anaerobic digestion of brewery spent grain (and co-digestion options) to produce biogas, and discusses use of nutrient‑enriched digestate/bioslurry as an agricultural input, including operational constraints and sustainability considerations.
WhyRelevant: Maps an 'energy + nutrients' valorisation route for BSG, helping prioritise configurations that improve waste cost, energy self-sufficiency and circularity outcomes.

Title: Impact of different production methods on selected aroma compounds for non-alcoholic beers by developing an HS-SPME GC–MS method
Authors: Sönke Kienitz; Hubert Kollmannsberger; Annsophie Schreff; Dennis Mayer; Christoph Neugrodda; Thomas Becker
AbstractMini: Compares production routes for non-alcoholic beer and validates an HS-SPME GC–MS workflow to quantify key aroma compounds; reports route-dependent shifts in volatiles relevant to sensory perception.
WhyRelevant: Direct lever for NOLO aroma QA/QC and process tuning (fermentation/dealcoholization trade-offs).
Owner: JORDI ROYES BUISAN;LUIS FERNANDO FRAGUA GONZALEZ

Title: Electrocoagulation of phosphates from alternating wastewaters utilising a hybrid set-up (conference session)
Authors: Noora Salonen; Kalle Salonen; Marko Suokas; Martti Latva
AbstractMini: Describes electrocoagulation of phosphates from alternating wastewaters using a hybrid electrode configuration (Al/Fe); reports removal efficiencies across different water matrices and operating conditions.
WhyRelevant: Directly applicable to brewery effluent phosphorus compliance; hybrid Al/Fe configuration may improve cost-efficiency over single-electrode systems for small-to-medium treatment plants.

Title: Postbiotics and the gut–brain axis: A mechanistic review on modulating neuroinflammation and cognitive aging
Authors: Rijhul Lahariya; Gargee Anand; Bandana Kumari; Ketan Priyadarshi
AbstractMini: Mechanistic review of microbiota-metabolite-neuroinflammation interactions, proposing postbiotics as a gut-brain axis modulation strategy with lower safety concerns than live probiotics.
WhyRelevant: Supports scientific rationale for postbiotic-enriched functional beverages targeting stress/anxiety; applicable to NOLO product claims and formulation strategy.
Owner: Javier Aldea Garcia;LUIS MIGUEL BARRERA ARBELAEZ

Title: Hypoglycemic effects under in vitro digestion of brewer's spent yeast β-glucan concentrate added to a rice extrudate
Authors: Marilin E. Aquino; Olga Martínez-Augustin; Fermín Sánchez de Medina; Franco Van de Velde; Silvina R. Drago; Raúl E. Cian
AbstractMini: Estudia productos derivados de β‑glucanos de levadura cervecera y su efecto hipoglucemiante in vitro (enzimas α‑amilasa/α‑glucosidasa/DPP‑IV).
WhyRelevant: Interesa para líneas de ingredientes funcionales desde BSY y claims/metodología de evaluación.

Title: Process ramanomics: single-cell Raman spectroscopy workflow for rapid monitoring of beer fermentation phenotypes
Authors: Liu Yang et al. (QIBEBT, Chinese Academy of Sciences)
AbstractMini: Develops a "process ramanomics" workflow using spontaneous single-cell Raman spectroscopy to predict 19 extracellular phenotypes (higher alcohols, esters, amino acids, sugars) during industrial lager fermentation, revealing cell-to-cell metabolic heterogeneity.
WhyRelevant: Pioneers label-free, single-cell PAT for brewing; links intracellular Raman fingerprints to conventional fermentation QC metrics, advancing real-time process monitoring beyond bulk methods.

Title: Synergistic toughening of biomass poly(ethylene furanoate) with CO2-derived poly(butylene carbonate) for sustainable materials
Authors: Bo Wang; Yunfei Wu; Lipeng Liu; Liyang Wang; Zhicheng Qiu; Zhiyong Li; Xu Qiu; Chang Xu; Xijing Zhuang
AbstractMini: Estudio experimental que mejora la tenacidad/compatibilidad de PEF mediante blending con PBC derivado de CO2, abordando fragilidad del PEF.
WhyRelevant: Relevante para packaging/biomateriales: rutas de formulación para mejorar performance sin perder narrativa de sostenibilidad.

Title: Membranes from bio-based poly(ethylene furanoate) and natural solvents (thymol/vanillin) for phase inversion
Authors: Malinalli Ramírez-Martínez; Usman T. Syed; Glenda Terán-Cuadrado; Anissa Nurdiawati; Maria Di Vincenzo; Iuliana M. Andrei; Dimitrios N. Bikiaris; Sami G. Al-Ghamdi; Suzana P. Nunes
AbstractMini: Propone fabricar membranas de PEF por inversión de fase usando un sistema solvente natural (thymol/vanillin) y reporta menor impacto climático vs PET comparable (según el estudio).
WhyRelevant: Conecta PEF con sostenibilidad de proceso (solventes) y aplicaciones de separación/filtración.

Title: Emerging smart packaging materials and technologies for ethylene detection and freshness monitoring
Authors: Pradeep Kumar; Shefali Tripathi; Ram Kumar Deshmukh; Bhushan P. Meshram; Lavanya; Kirtiraj K. Gaikwad
AbstractMini: Reviews smart packaging technologies for real-time freshness monitoring and ethylene detection; covers colorimetric indicators, biosensors, RFID/NFC integration, and emerging sensing materials.
WhyRelevant: Directly relevant to active/smart packaging roadmap for beer and beverage freshness assurance; identifies sensor technologies compatible with food-contact packaging.

Title: A review of innovative approaches for valorizing brewing by-products for food applications and consumer perceptions
Authors: A. Halahlah; A. Kellil; S. Peltonen; T. M. Ho
AbstractMini: Reviews recent extraction, bioprocessing and product development strategies to incorporate brewing solid by-products (BSG, BSY, spent hops) into food, and evaluates consumer perception factors influencing market acceptance.
WhyRelevant: Bridges the gap between technical valorization advances and market viability by incorporating consumer-acceptance data alongside processing innovation for brewing by-products.

Title: Alternative Configurations for the Intensification of the Anaerobic Digestion Process: A Comprehensive Review
Authors: Juan García Rodríguez (et al.)
AbstractMini: Reviews alternative configurations for intensifying anaerobic digestion: thermophilic, two-stage acid/gas, temperature-phased, and multi-stage systems; covers mechanisms, key factors, and scalability.
WhyRelevant: Framework for evaluating AD intensification options for brewery wastewater and BSG co-digestion; two-stage and temperature-phased designs are directly transferable to medium-scale brewery biogas.

Title: UK land use implications of replacing dairy farming with precision fermentation
Authors: Jade A. Warren; E. R. Fonseca; H. Woodward
AbstractMini: Quantifies land-use implications of scaling precision-fermented dairy protein in the UK, estimating up to 96 % lower land footprint vs. cow's milk and modelling 20–100 % replacement scenarios for 2050.
WhyRelevant: Provides macro-level sustainability evidence for PF ingredients; relevant to breweries evaluating PF-derived adjuncts or dairy-alternative functional beverages as part of ESG strategy.

Title: Toward a digital twin for beer quality control: development of a digital model integrating industrial process data and model-based fermentation descriptors
Authors: Colomba Dazzarola; Ronald Tighe; José R. Pérez-Correa; Pedro A. Saa
AbstractMini: Presents a hybrid digital model for beer quality prediction that combines industrial process data, a dynamic fermentation model and machine-learning classifiers. The system identified wort preparation and yeast handling as critical drivers of final quality attributes.
WhyRelevant: Useful for building process-control and prediction layers linking fermentation signals to beer quality, defects and corrective actions in production.

Title: In vitro protein digestibility and protein quality assessment of novel brewers' spent grain protein isolates
Authors: Arianna Ressa; Laura Nyhan; Rosa Sanchez; Dustie N. Lombardi; Patrick O'Riordan; Elke K. Arendt
AbstractMini: Assesses digestibility and DIAAS of two upcycled brewers' spent grain protein ingredients versus pea, soy and whey isolates using the INFOGEST model. Both ingredients showed high digestibility, but decolourisation appeared to reduce protein quality.
WhyRelevant: Helps judge whether BSG protein isolates are viable ingredients for nutrition-led products and where processing may erode functional or nutritional value.

Title: Extraction of proteins from brewer's spent grain using ultrasound and alkaline hydrogen peroxide: Impact on extraction yield, structural and techno-functional properties
Authors: Marcio Augusto Ribeiro-Sanches; Sérgio Luís Ferreira Júnior; Caroline Santezi; Ícaro Putinhon Caruso; Pedro Esteves Duarte Augusto; Javier Telis-Romero; Tiago Carregari Polachini
AbstractMini: Evaluates ultrasound and alkaline hydrogen peroxide to intensify protein extraction from brewer's spent grain. The treatments increased extraction yield and improved foaming and emulsifying properties, with trade-offs in color and water-holding capacity depending on the route.
WhyRelevant: Relevant for designing scalable BSG protein recovery routes and matching extracted fractions to beverage, foam or food-ingredient applications.

Title: Hordein reclaimed from brewer’s spent grains (spent malt) for fabrication of plant oil based pork fat tissue analogues
Authors: Wenqing Yang; Xianjian Zeng; Yi Lin; Dejian Huang
AbstractMini: Reclaims hordein from brewer's spent grain to build emulsion gels that mimic pork fat tissue. The gels showed elastic structure, controlled oil release and cooking losses comparable to pork fat, with post-heating improving texture.
WhyRelevant: Shows a higher-value route for BSG proteins beyond feed, with relevance for ingredient licensing, co-products and alternative-food partnerships.

Title: Brewers' spent grain reborn via cellulase–xylanase hydrolysis and Lactobacillus acidophilus fermentation and their application in bread
Authors: Kexun Liu; Zhengjie Xie; Yan Huang; Huayi Suo; Guohua Zhao; Damao Wang
AbstractMini: Uses cellulase-xylanase hydrolysis followed by Lactobacillus acidophilus fermentation to valorize brewer's spent grain. The treatment improved phenolics, hydration and soluble protein, and enabled bread fortification up to 15% without major sensory penalties.
WhyRelevant: Demonstrates enzyme-plus-fermentation upgrading of BSG into more functional ingredients, with transferable logic for circular ingredient platforms.

Title: Nitrogen and phosphorus recovery by struvite: From fundamental challenges to engineering strategies
Authors: Mingye Zhang; Junfeng Liang; Keqiang Zhang; Fuyuan Liu; Xingliang Gao; Zhuowu Li; Yingjun Ma; Xingping Chang; Mengya Wang; Lianzhu Du
AbstractMini: Reviews struvite crystallization for simultaneous nitrogen and phosphorus recovery, focusing on pH, magnesium sources, feed variability and scale-up constraints. It also covers contaminant control, pretreatment and process intensification options for industrial deployment.
WhyRelevant: Frames nutrient-recovery options relevant to brewery effluents and helps assess where struvite could fit as a water-circularity and cost-reduction lever.
